_readme: TRUE PUNE-DISTRICT-SPECIFIC data (not state-pooled) — extracted directly from Volume II 'Crop wise District wise Cost of Cultivation Results', Directorate of Economics & Statistics, Government of Maharashtra. Each table below is the actual Pune district row from the primary survey, reference year 2018-19, uploaded by Bade as Collection_Volume_II_Final.pdf. 1 hectare = 2.471 acres — acre figures are direct conversions.
critical_caveats: Still dated 2018-19 — needs an inflation/escalation adjustment before real use, same as Volume I data. Not yet applied.

Yield ('Output Main Produce') below is a single average figure from the Pune sample for that crop, not a min-max range — our engine's CropBaseline schema wants a range. Until we have real season-to-season variability data, treat this figure as the CENTER of a band we're estimating, not a measured range. Flagged for the next integration step, not silently converted into a fake range.

8 of Pune's crop list were surveyed in Pune district directly: Onion, Tomato, Wheat, Gram, Paddy(Rice), Jowar, Sugarcane, Pomegranate. Tur, Cotton, Turmeric, Groundnut, Grapes, Banana, and Fig were NOT surveyed in Pune specifically in this study (surveyed in other districts instead) — for those we still fall back to the Maharashtra state-level figures already captured in pune-cost-of-cultivation-2018-19-SOURCED.json.

'Per Quintal Cost' (row 27 of each table) is Cost C net of by-product value divided by main-produce output — this is the single most useful number for our profit engine's cost-per-quintal comparison against mandi price.

Onion.note: Cost C (114,283/ha) far exceeds revenue (62,366/ha) — Pune onion recorded a substantial LOSS in the 2018-19 survey year. Matches the state-level finding that onion had negative returns that year, but the loss is even starker at Pune-specific level.

Tomato.note: This is the exact table behind the 'Pune has the lowest tomato price in Maharashtra' finding from Volume I — ₹638/qtl realized. Revenue (173,370) still well below Cost C (203,608), a loss-making year for tomato in Pune too.

Wheat.note: This is the exact table behind 'Pune has the lowest wheat cultivation cost in Maharashtra' — genuinely profitable: Cost C 35,135 vs revenue ~58,907 (incl. by-product).

Gram_Chana.note: Profitable in Pune: revenue 91,463 vs Cost C 53,545.

Rice_Bhat.note: Paddy (Kharif). Profitable in Pune: revenue ~99,906 (incl. by-product) vs Cost C 71,702.

Jowar_Sorghum.note: Kharif Jowar. No family labour or irrigation cost recorded in the Pune sample specifically — rainfed, hired-labour-only cultivation pattern here. Profitable: revenue ~61,353 (incl. by-product) vs Cost C 40,113.

Sugarcane.note: Very fertilizer/manure-heavy cost structure (39,420 + 20,313 = ~28% of Cost A alone) — matches Volume I's national note that fertilizer+manure is ~29% of sugarcane's cost. Profitable: revenue 294,308 vs Cost C 215,721.

Pomegranate.note: Perennial (Purandar taluka specialty). Plant protection is by far the largest single cost (47,321/ha, ~27% of Cost A) — pomegranate is notoriously pest/disease-prone (bacterial blight, fruit borer), this shows up directly in the real cost structure. Very profitable: revenue 327,933 vs Cost C 261,397.
